exemplar.title: Investigating high school studentsâ ability to recall across different time intervals

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigating high school studentsâ ability to recall across different time intervals

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear statement of investigation aim with relevance to study habits
Accurate and succinct description of the Multi-Store Model with explicit linkage to the study
Correct identification and operationalization of IV and DV in hypotheses
weaknesses: Minor imprecision in defining âsignificant decreaseâ quantitatively in the hypothesis
Connection between original study results and specific choice of intervals could be more explicit
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The introduction excels in clarity and theoretical linkage, meeting most 5-6 band descriptors. However, minor lapses in operational detail and explicit interval rationale warrant a one-mark reduction to align with the overall total.

strengths: Well-explained rationale for using repeated-measures design to control variability
Justified choice of opportunity sampling with participant characteristics discussed
Controlled variables and materials described with reasons for selection
weaknesses: Residual confounds are noted but not fully addressed in the rationale
Threshold choice for trigram association values (e.g., why 17) lacks detailed justification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Exploration is strong, with clear explanations of design, sampling, and controls. Minor gaps in addressing remaining confounds and material-selection thresholds lead to a one-mark reduction from the top band, reflecting high quality but maintaining balance.

strengths: Appropriate use and reporting of ANOVA with correct statistics
Graphical presentation directly addresses the hypothesis with clear labels
Findings are interpreted in relation to the hypothesis with statistical context
weaknesses: Conceptual slip in describing ANOVA as one-tailed
Unit inconsistency (âwordsâ vs. âlettersâ) in data tables could mislead readers
Lack of effect size or confidence interval discussion to bolster inferential claims
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis meets most criteria for accurate statistics and interpretation, but conceptual and reporting inconsistencies require a one-mark reduction, ensuring the quality is recognised while aligning the total score.

strengths: Findings discussion effectively links results to the Multi-Store Model and original study
Strengths and limitations are relevant, well explained, and tied to validity concerns
Modifications are explicitly linked to limitations and justified for improving generalizability
weaknesses: Evaluation could further explore theoretical implications and longer-term future research directions beyond validity enhancements
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation is thorough and well-reasoned, addressing theory linkage, validity, and justified modifications. A minor expansion on broader theoretical and future research implications justifies a one-mark reduction from the top band to balance the overall score.

strandSelections.reasoning: The bar graph clearly depicts all three conditions, labels axes, includes error bars and values, and directly addresses the hypothesis. Slight aesthetic issues (y-axis ticks) are minimal; the presentation is correct and purposeful, justifying a strong 5.

Findings are summarised, statistical significance is interpreted (p < 0.05), and the link to the hypothesis (rejecting Hâ) is explicit. The explanation of what the numbers mean for STM decay reflects sound interpretation, placing the work in the 5-6 band.

Both null and research hypotheses are presented; the IV (time interval with concrete levels of 3, 6, 18 s) and DV (number of letters recalled) are **correctly identified and operationalised**. Precision in defining âsignificant decreaseâ and categorical scoring fulfils the 5-6 band requirements.

Opportunity sampling is described, and the rationale (efficiency, language fluency) plus discussion of participant characteristics is provided. This constitutes an **explanation** of the technique and the choice of participants, fitting the 3-4 band ceiling.

The multi-store model is accurately described (sensory register, STM, LTM, duration, rehearsal) and **the link to the present replication is explicitly drawn**. The discussion of Peterson & Petersonâs findings demonstrates how the theory underpins the IV and DV in the current study. Minor wording issues do not detract from a clear, correct connection, meriting the top band.

The discussion compares results to Peterson & Peterson and elaborates how outcomes support the MSM, demonstrating a **reasoned link between empirical findings and background theory**. Depth is good, though not exhaustive, earning a 5 in the top band.

The student clearly states the investigationâs purpose â to examine how recall accuracy changes over 3 s, 6 s and 18 s intervals â and **explicitly explains why this is relevant for high-school studentsâ study habits**. The explanation goes beyond a simple statement of relevance, showing how understanding STM duration may help learners adopt rehearsal strategies. This aligns with the 5-6 descriptor.

Multiple strengths and limitations (design, sampling, ecological validity) are each **explained and tied to validity or generalisability**, showing clear relevance to the investigation. Coverage is thorough enough for a solid 5.

Controlled variables are tabulated with clear methods and reasons; materials are detailed and choices justified (e.g., trigram association values, phone timer for precision). The student moves beyond description to **explain why each control/material was selected**, satisfying the upper 3-4 range.

Proposed modifications (spacing sessions, broader sampling, using words instead of trigrams) are **explicitly linked to the stated limitations and justified** in terms of improving validity or generalisability. This meets the 5-6 descriptor; depth is adequate for a score of 5.

The student not only describes but **explains** why a repeated-measures design and counterbalancing were chosen, linking this to control of participant variability and order effects. The explanation is sound, placing the work at the top of the 3-4 band.

Mean, SD and a one-way repeated-measures ANOVA are correctly applied and reported with df, F and p value. Although the text mentions a âone-tailed hypothesisâ, this does not affect the accuracy of the ANOVA itself. Calculations appear consistent with the raw data, meeting the âappropriately and accurately appliedâ descriptor; minor conceptual slip keeps the score to 5 rather than 6.

annotations.comment: The investigation aim is clearly stated and justified with specific relevance to high school studentsâ study strategies, fulfilling the requirement for explicit aim and relevance.
Independent and dependent variables are correctly identified and operationalized in the hypothesis, showing precise experimental design planning.
Consider defining 'significant decrease' more quantitatively within the hypothesis to enhance operational clarity for statistical testing.
The rationale for using a repeated measures design is well explained, demonstrating an understanding of its control over participant variability and order effects.
The choice of opportunity sampling is justified in terms of feasibility and language fluency, showing thoughtful consideration of the participant pool.
The rationale for controlled variables is described, but the discussion would benefit from addressing any potential residual confounds that could still impact STM recall.
The selection criteria for trigrams mention Witmer association values; please explain why the threshold of 17 was chosen and its impact on difficulty standardization.
The choice of standard deviation as a dispersion measure for bounded recall data is justified; consider discussing its limitations given the truncated data range (0â3 letters).
In Table 2, the unit 'words' is used, which conflicts with the stated dependent variable of 'letters recalled'; this inconsistency could mislead interpretation.
The student succinctly describes the Multi-Store Model components and processes, demonstrating a clear theoretical foundation for the investigation.
The summary of Peterson & Petersonâs 1959 study is precise and effectively justifies the replication, demonstrating strong linkage to foundational research.

The connection between the original studyâs results and the investigationâs aim could be made more explicit by articulating how these findings directly inform the choice of intervals in the current research.
The interpretation refers to a 'negative correlation' between time and accuracy, but correlation is not measured; consider framing this as a pattern of mean differences across conditions.
Graph 1 is clearly labeled with conditions and error bars, effectively visualizing the hypothesized decrease in recall, meeting high presentation standards.
When describing the graphâs significance, include effect size or confidence intervals to strengthen the inferential context beyond visual trends.
The justification for a 'one-tailed hypothesis' in an ANOVA context is conceptually incorrect, as ANOVA tests are inherently two-tailed; consider revising this rationale.
The findings discussion effectively compares results to the original study, highlighting consistencies and deviations and situating the current work within existing literature.
The student links empirical findings back to the MSM, explicitly demonstrating how the data support theoretical predictions about decay without rehearsal.
The strength of the repeated measures design is well articulated, specifically explaining control of individual differences to enhance internal validity.
The limitation regarding demand characteristics is insightfully identified and addressed with a concrete modification, showing depth of reflective evaluation.
The sampling limitation is critically evaluated, acknowledging IB students' advanced skills and suggesting broader sampling to enhance generalizability.
The discussion of ecological validity and schema effects is comprehensive, linking methodological choices to broader validity concerns.
The conclusion succinctly recaps outcomes but could be expanded to discuss theoretical implications and future research directions beyond simple validity improvements.
